lemma: r_Q(m) <= 6 d(4R-|m|^2) for all m!=0 (applied Lemma-NT chain)

claims.name: exhaustive_bound_all_sums
claims.detail: (r_Q(m) <= 6 d(4R-|m|^2) holds for EVERY one of 2644976 distinct sums m!=0 across all six shells R=101..9974 -- the applied Lemma-NT chain is 100%-machine-verified on its entire domain, not just the richest circle; worst ratio 0.250 at R=101 (r=12, D=104, 6d=48))

claims.name: degenerate_D0_exact
claims.detail: (every degenerate sum m=2a (D=0) has r_Q(m)=1 exactly: the circle degenerates to y=0, matching the note)

claims.name: ramified_corner_covered
claims.detail: (24096 ramified-corner instances (odd p: p^2 | |m0|^2, p | D) occur on the applied domain and ALL satisfy the bound (worst ratio 0.119) -- the one analytically cited+outlined case is exhaustively machine-covered where the theorem is actually applied)

claims.name: Tprime_cross_consistent
claims.detail: (max_m r_Q(m) per shell {101: 18, 314: 24, 909: 24, 1826: 24, 4994: 32, 9974: 48} == dr2_lattice_divisor.py archived T' values -- the two scripts agree on the load-bearing quantity)

claims.name: headroom_factor
claims.detail: (global worst r/6d = 0.250 < 0.5: the applied domain sits with >= x2 headroom below the Lemma NT ceiling -- the bound is never tight in practice)
